title: codariocalyx gyroides
intro: Codariocalyx gyroides, also known as the climbing milk vine or gyroides, is a perennial vine native to Southeast Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Codariocalyx gyroides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with some traditional practitioners also using it as a poultice or applying the plant's extract topically to affected areas.
